evals.prompt: Refactor OrderService.java to follow our Java 8 coding conventions. The class has mutable state, uses loops instead of streams, throws checked exceptions, uses null returns, and has methods doing too many things.
evals.expected_output: Refactored OrderService with: final fields, constructor injection, streams instead of loops, Optional instead of null, unchecked exceptions, small focused methods, all variables final

assertions.text: All local variables and parameters use final keyword
No checked exceptions in method signatures
No for/while loops — all collection processing uses streams
Methods that may not find a result return Optional instead of null
No method exceeds ~15 lines of logic

evals.prompt: Refactor UserProfile.java to follow our Java 8 conventions. It's a mutable JavaBean with getters/setters and imperative collection code.
evals.expected_output: Immutable UserProfile with: private final fields, constructor sets everything, no setters, unmodifiable collection return, streams for collection methods, all variables final

assertions.text: All local variables and parameters use final keyword
Single constructor that accepts and sets all fields

evals.prompt: Write a Java 8 service class called InventoryService that manages a product inventory. It should support: finding products by category, calculating total stock value, finding the most expensive product, and getting a summary of stock levels grouped by category. Use a ProductRepository dependency for data access.
evals.expected_output: New class following all conventions: final fields, constructor injection, streams for all collection ops, Optional for find methods, small focused methods, no loops, all variables final

assertions.text: All local variables and parameters use final keyword
All fields on InventoryService are private final
